blurb: More than 300 arcade and pinball machines, retro and new console systems, tabletop games, and tournaments all under one roof for a full weekend. The Southern-Fried Gaming Expo at the Marriott Renaissance Waverly is the kind of thing that sounds like it is for someone else until you realize how many people in East Cobb grew up on Pac-Man and Atari and have been low-key looking for an excuse to play them again. Teens will love it. Parents who grew up in the 80s will really love it. Tickets go on sale, and the expo is July 31 through August 2.
scoring_notes: Fun and genuinely unique event. Good fit for the East Cobb family-with-teens demographic. However, the event is 10+ weeks out, which is too far for a 'this week' newsletter feature. Excluded from final output on timing grounds.

blurb: The Georgia Renaissance Festival is still running through the end of May, which means you have two more weekends to catch it before it wraps up for the year. It is held in Fairburn, so plan for a 45-minute drive from East Cobb, but for a lot of families this is an annual tradition. Think jousting, costumed performers, roasted turkey legs, artisan vendors, and enough spectacle to keep kids and adults both entertained for most of the day. The season ends May 31, so if you have been putting it off, this is the nudge.
scoring_notes: Annual event with a firm end date creates real urgency. Fairburn is a significant drive from East Cobb, which is a trade-off. Good fit for families with teens. East Cobb income level handles ticket prices easily. The 'last chance' angle gives it a stronger push than a mid-season mention would.

date: Saturday (specific date not confirmed in listing)
blurb: 46-foot ornamental boats. Drum-driven racing. Lake Allatoona as the backdrop. The Acworth-Cobb Dragon Boat Festival is one of those events that sounds niche until you actually show up and realize how genuinely fun it is to watch. The races draw on centuries of Chinese tradition, and this local version also raises money for cancer patients and survivors in the community, which gives it some real weight beyond just the spectacle. Easy to spend a few hours here with family. Worth checking the website for exact date and times before you go.
scoring_notes: High uniqueness, visually compelling, community-oriented. The listing references 'this Saturday' but the source listicle appears to be from a May 1 weekend roundup, so the event may have already occurred. DATE VERIFICATION NEEDED. The candidate summary does not confirm the event date falls on or after May 22. Flagging as uncertain.

blurb: If you like browsing art without a hard sell and a good excuse to get outside, this one is worth the short drive up to Alpharetta. The Arts Streetfest fills Wills Park with artisans, vendors, street performers, and kids activities across the whole weekend. It is the kind of event you can drop into for an hour or make a half-day out of, depending on how into it you get. Free to attend, low pressure, and easy to bring the whole family or just go with a neighbor. Nice way to kick off a long weekend.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for East Cobb's educated, established-family and empty-nester mix. Free entry removes any barrier. Arts festivals draw well from this income bracket. Not a rare event type, but it is a specific annual event with a clear date and confirmed timing within the publication window.
